title: Serum ferritin, neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio, and lymphocyte-to-monocyte ratio are associated with levodopa-induced dyskinesia severity in Parkinson's disease.
abstract: Levodopa-induced dyskinesia (LID) is a common motor complication of Parkinson's disease (PD), yet peripheral biological factors associated with dyskinesia severity remain insufficiently characterized. Systemic inflammation and iron dysregulation have been implicated in PD, but their relationship with LID expression is unclear. We investigated the association between serum ferritin, neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR), lymphocyte-to-monocyte ratio (LMR), and the presence and severity of LID.
